topStories.headline: 76ers Shock Celtics Without Embiid — Edgecombe and Maxey Deliver Stunning G2 Win
body: The Philadelphia 76ers, playing without Joel Embiid for the second straight game following his emergency appendectomy, stunned the Boston Celtics 111-97 in Game 2 to even their first-round series 1-1. Rookie V.J. Edgecombe was breathtaking — 30 points and 10 rebounds in a breakout performance — while Tyrese Maxey added 29 to shoulder the floor general load in Embiid's absence. The Celtics, who had won Game 1 comfortably 123-91, were outplayed in every phase at TD Garden. The series shifts to Philadelphia for Game 3 on Friday, and the defining question of this matchup is now when — or whether — Embiid can return.

topStories.headline: Lightning Survive OT, Bruins Rally to Even Their Series — East's Upsets Contained
body: Two teams that stumbled in Game 1 responded emphatically Tuesday night. J.J. Moser scored in overtime to give the Tampa Bay Lightning a 3-2 win over the Montreal Canadiens — Moser became the first Lightning player to score his first career playoff goal in overtime, a genuinely rare moment. In Boston, Viktor Arvidsson and Morgan Geekie led a composed Bruins effort in a 4-2 win over the Buffalo Sabres. Both series are now 1-1, with Games 3 and 4 heading to the underdogs' buildings — Tampa and Boston traveling to face hostile crowds in Montreal and Buffalo.

topStories.headline: Cobolli Dismantles World No. 1 Zverev in Munich Semis — Shelton Final Sunday
body: Flavio Cobolli delivered one of the clay-court season's biggest upsets, dispatching top seed Alexander Zverev 6-3, 6-3 with 32 winners in the Munich ATP 500 semifinals. Zverev, the world number one, had no answers for the Italian's relentless forehand. Cobolli will face Ben Shelton in Sunday's final — Shelton eased past qualifier Alex Molcan 6-3, 6-4 in the other semi. A Shelton win on Sunday would make him the first American man to claim an ATP 500+ clay title since Andre Agassi in Rome in 2002.

topStories.headline: Six Playoff Games on Deck — OKC Eyes 2-0, Battle of PA Returns to Pittsburgh
body: Wednesday's schedule delivers six playoff games across both leagues. In the NBA, the Magic visit Detroit for Game 2 (7 ET, ESPN) — Orlando pulled off the G1 upset and now faces a hostile Pistons crowd. The Thunder host Phoenix in Game 2 (9:30 ET, ESPN): OKC torched the Suns by 35 in Game 1 and look to seize a 2-0 lead. The Lakers visit Houston for Game 2 with Kevin Durant's availability uncertain. In the NHL, the Flyers go to Pittsburgh for Game 3 (7 ET, TNT) with a chance to put the Penguins on the brink, the Stars visit Minnesota for G3 (9:30 ET, TNT), and the Oilers host Anaheim in G2 (10 ET, TBS).

stories.headline: Sixers Stun Celtics, Trail Blazers Even Series — Four 1-1 Ties Across the Bracket
body: The night's signature moment came in Boston, where the 76ers — Embiid-less — beat the Celtics 111-97 to level their series. V.J. Edgecombe's 30-point, 10-rebound performance and Tyrese Maxey's 29-point masterclass made Philadelphia impossible to guard without their superstar center. The Celtics, who had been dominant in Game 1, were simply outworked. The Portland Trail Blazers also tied their series with the San Antonio Spurs 1-1, reclaiming home-court advantage. The first-round bracket now features four 1-1 series — Boston-Philly, Atlanta-New York, Minnesota-Denver, and Portland-San Antonio — creating maximum chaos heading into Games 3 and 4.

stories.headline: KD Questionable Tonight, Embiid in Recovery — Luka Also Out for Lakers
body: Kevin Durant is a game-time decision for tonight's Lakers-Rockets Game 2 — he missed Game 1 entirely, and Houston was blunted in his absence. The Lakers are simultaneously without Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves, making tonight's game a hobbled affair on both ends. Joel Embiid remains the first-round's central health storyline: his appendectomy on April 9 has already cost him two games, and the average recovery window suggests his return — if it comes in this series — would be for Games 4 or 5. The Sixers proved Tuesday that they can compete; the question is whether Boston adjusts.

stories.headline: Lightning and Bruins Even Their Series — Underdogs' Early Leads Wiped Out
body: Two of the East's early 'upset' series reset to 1-1 Tuesday night. J.J. Moser's overtime goal gave the Tampa Bay Lightning a 3-2 Game 2 win over the Montreal Canadiens — the first career playoff goal for the defenseman, delivered in the most dramatic setting possible. Brandon Hagel and Nikita Kucherov also scored for Tampa. In Boston, the Bruins methodically pulled away from the Sabres 4-2, with Viktor Arvidsson, Morgan Geekie, and Pavel Zacha all finding the net. Both series now move to the lower seed's buildings for Games 3 and 4, where Montreal and Buffalo will try to reclaim ground.

stories.headline: Battle of PA G3 in Pittsburgh — Flyers Can Put Penguins on the Brink
body: The Philadelphia Flyers, leading the Pittsburgh Penguins 2-0, travel to PPG Paints Arena tonight for Game 3 (7 ET, TNT). A third Flyers win would push Sidney Crosby and the Penguins to the edge of elimination — a stunning outcome for a storied franchise. The Dallas Stars visit the Minnesota Wild for Game 3 (9:30 ET, TNT) in a series tied 1-1 — home ice now belongs to Minnesota. Later, the defending-champion Edmonton Oilers host the Anaheim Ducks for Game 2 (10 ET, TBS), with Connor McDavid expected to push for a 2-0 series advantage.

stories.headline: Astros Cruise Past Guardians 8-2 as Early-Season Division Races Take Shape
body: The Houston Astros handled the Cleveland Guardians 8-2 Tuesday night, with Houston's lineup clicking across the board as the AL Central race begins to crystallize. The Miami Marlins also won, beating the St. Louis Cardinals 4-2. With the NBA and NHL playoffs commanding attention, MLB hums productively in the background — the Los Angeles Dodgers remain the NL's benchmark team, with Shohei Ohtani anchoring a lineup that has been near-unstoppable. The season is approaching its one-quarter mark, and meaningful division separations are beginning to form across both leagues.

stories.headline: PSG vs. Bayern, Arsenal vs. Atlético — First Legs Six Days Away
body: The UEFA Champions League semifinals arrive in six days with two absorbing matchups. PSG — who swept Liverpool 4-0 on aggregate — host Bayern Munich on April 28 at the Parc des Princes. Bayern advanced in one of the quarterfinal stage's great comebacks, overturning Real Madrid 6-4 on aggregate across two legs. On April 29, Atlético Madrid host Arsenal in Madrid — Diego Simeone's defensive organization versus Mikel Arteta's high press in what should be a tactical chess match. Kai Havertz has been Arsenal's clutch UCL performer; Harry Kane and Michael Olise lead Bayern's attack. Return legs May 5–6. Final: Budapest, May 30.

stories.headline: F1 Championship Paused Until Miami — Antonelli Leads Russell by Nine Points
body: Formula 1's 2026 season has an extended break in April after the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix were cancelled due to regional instability. The championship resumes at the Miami Grand Prix (May 1–3). Teenager Kimi Antonelli leads the standings at 72 points after his Chinese GP victory, with Mercedes teammate George Russell nine points back at 63. Charles Leclerc (49 pts) keeps Ferrari in the conversation. The break gives teams additional development time as the season heads into a busy Americas swing.

stories.headline: Cobolli Destroys Zverev — Shelton vs. Cobolli Final Set for Sunday
body: Flavio Cobolli struck 32 winners to dismantle top seed Alexander Zverev 6-3, 6-3 in the BMW Open Munich semifinals Wednesday — a dominant, clean display from the Italian that gave the world number one no foothold. He'll face Ben Shelton in Sunday's ATP 500 final: Shelton dispatched qualifier Alex Molcan 6-3, 6-4 in the other semi, losing just six points on his first serve. A Shelton title Sunday would make him the first American man to win an ATP 500+ clay event since Agassi in Rome (2002). The Barcelona Open is also at the semifinal stage this week, and the Mutua Madrid Open (ATP Masters 1000 / WTA 1000) continues through May 3.

stories.headline: Team Event Opens Thursday at TPC Louisiana — Fitzpatrick Brothers Among Favorites
body: The Zurich Classic of New Orleans — the PGA Tour's only team competition — begins Thursday at TPC Louisiana in Avondale, running through Sunday. The 74-team, 148-player field plays alternate-shot and better-ball rounds in a unique format that rewards chemistry as much as individual brilliance. Matt and Alex Fitzpatrick enter as one of the most talked-about pairings after Matt's dramatic RBC Heritage playoff win last Sunday over world No. 1 Scheffler. Brooks Koepka and Shane Lowry are among other notable teams in the field.

stories.headline: Dover Looms After Reddick's Historic Kansas Performance
body: Following Tyler Reddick's five-wins-in-nine-races historic Kansas run last Sunday, the NASCAR Cup Series prepares for Dover Motor Speedway next weekend. Reddick's 2026 pace has placed him in elite company — only four drivers in NASCAR history have managed five wins in the first nine Cup races of a season. Dover's Monster Mile is a different test entirely: a one-mile concrete oval that punishes mechanical issues and demands precise tire management. Whether Reddick can maintain his extraordinary pace on a track this demanding will be one of the weekend's central storylines.
